name: Nereus
description: Nereus is an early sea god in Greek mythology, known as the 'Old Man of the Sea.' He is the son of Pontus (the Sea) and Gaia (the Earth), making him a primordial deity. Nereus is generally depicted as a wise, benevolent, and truthful figure, unlike other sea gods who are often portrayed as capricious or dangerous. He possesses the power of prophecy and the ability to change his shape at will, which he uses to evade those seeking his wisdom. He is married to Doris, daughter of Oceanus and Tethys, and together they have fifty daughters known as the Nereids, sea nymphs celebrated for their beauty and helpfulness to sailors. These Nereids include Amphitrite, who became the wife of Poseidon. Nereus represents the calm and fertile aspects of the sea, embodying knowledge and understanding of its depths. He resides in a cave at the bottom of the Aegean Sea and is often consulted by heroes and mortals seeking guidance. While Poseidon eventually became the dominant sea god, Nereus remained a respected and significant figure in Greek mythology, representing the wisdom and ancient power of the ocean.
subtitle: The Wise Old Man of the Sea

keyMyths.title: Heracles and Nereus
keyMyths.description: Heracles sought Nereus to learn the location of the Garden of the Hesperides. Nereus, a shapeshifter, was wrestled into revealing the information.

keyMyths.title: Nereus's Prophecies
keyMyths.description: Nereus was known for his prophetic abilities, often consulted for insights into the future. His prophecies were considered reliable and truthful.

keyMyths.title: Parentage of the Nereids
keyMyths.description: Nereus and Doris are the parents of the fifty Nereids, beautiful sea nymphs who embody the allure and bounty of the sea.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: A major source of Greek mythology, including the genealogy of the gods.
sources.text: A major source of Greek mythology, including the genealogy of the gods.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Greek myths and legends.

sources.description: A collection of short summaries of Greek and Roman myths.
